description: Complete a document-grounded extraction and verification task from the imported context. Source candidate intent: Reusable skill for searching academic databases, extracting metadata, validating citations, and converting DOIs to BibTeX.. The Skill returns a structured answer, extracted steps, evidence, and verifier notes; it does not perform self-play training, clone repositories, install packages, download datasets, or mutate local files by itself.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: You are an expert citation manager. For the given action and query, access the appropriate academic source (Google Scholar, PubMed, CrossRef via DOI), retrieve accurate metadata, validate or convert as needed, and return the result in the specified format. Follow standard academic citation rules and BibTeX guidelines.

action.description: The type of citation management operation to perform.

query.description: Search query or citation text or DOI.

metadata.description: Structured metadata (authors, title, etc.) for extract action.

result.description: BibTeX string, corrected citation, or validation report.

postconditions: If action is 'search', one or more results are returned.
If action is 'convert_doi', a valid BibTeX entry is generated.
If action is 'validate', discrepancy list or confirmation of correctness is provided.
preconditions: If action is 'search' or 'convert_doi', the paper must exist in the targeted source.

name: document_grounded_extractor_document_skillsbench_06_skill_citation_management

challenges.expected_evidence: A BibTeX string that when processed by a BibTeX parser yields the correct metadata for the paper.
challenges.failure_signal: Output is not valid BibTeX, or one or more fields are missing/inaccurate.
challenges.task: Given a DOI '10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2', retrieve the full BibTeX entry for the corresponding paper.

challenges.expected_evidence: A corrected citation string and explanation of discrepancies.
challenges.failure_signal: Fails to identify significant errors or outputs an incorrect correction.
rubric: Identifies at least two errors (year, title, journal, volume, pages).
Provides corrected citation in APA format.
challenges.task: Given a user-provided citation 'Smith, J. (2020). A study on AI. Journal of AI, 15(2), 100-110.' validate it and correct any errors. Assume the correct citation is 'Smith, J., & Lee, K. (2021). A study on artificial intelligence. Journal of Artificial Intelligence, 16(3), 200-210.'

constraints: Only use authoritative academic sources (Google Scholar, PubMed).
BibTeX output must strictly follow BibTeX syntax.
Metadata must be accurate and complete.
examples: Input DOI '10.1000/xyz123' -> Output BibTeX '@article{key, author={...}, title={...}, journal={...}, year=...}'
facts: The skill can search Google Scholar for academic papers.
The skill can search PubMed for academic papers.
The skill can extract accurate metadata from papers.
The skill can validate citation information.
The skill can generate properly formatted BibTeX entries.
The skill can convert DOIs to BibTeX format.
procedures: To search: provide a query string to Google Scholar or PubMed API endpoint.
To extract: retrieve full metadata (authors, title, journal, year, DOI) from the search result or DOI.
To validate: compare citation fields against authoritative sources and flag discrepancies.
To generate BibTeX: map metadata to standard BibTeX fields and format according to style guidelines.
To convert DOI: resolve DOI to metadata via CrossRef or similar, then generate BibTeX.
context_pack.summary: A skill for comprehensive citation management in academic research, enabling search of Google Scholar and PubMed, extraction of metadata, validation of citations, and generation of BibTeX entries.

ctx2skill_evidence.demo_scope: P0/P1 demo-paper implementation; not full long-horizon self-play training.

judge_results.rationale: Ctx2Skill-lite replay score meets the demo threshold.

# SkillsBench citation-management skill documentation

Source path: `tasks/citation-check/environment/skills/citation-management/SKILL.md`

---
name: citation-management
description: Comprehensive citation management for academic research. Search Google Scholar and PubMed for papers, extract accurate metadata, validate citations, and generate properly formatted BibTeX entries. This skill should be used when you need to find papers, verify citation information, convert DOIs to BibTeX, or ensure r
